subject: [CONTRIB] Add new external-schema-repo-generator tool
commit_message:
[CONTRIB] Add new external-schema-repo-generator tool

Added script tool which generates and installs ConfigMap
containing schemas used for event validation. Main purpose
of this script is mounting custom schemas to DCAE VES Collector,
yet it might be used in any component.

Signed-off-by: Michal Banka <michal.banka@nokia.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-2589
Change-Id: I29dfe189c61b913ff66fcc5248fe9d4026ac9a2a

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 2:

NO issues in Gating, can we merge this?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I didn't looked at all the patch as I'm not sure to understand exactly why we need it and why we cannot use some ONAP APIs in order to achieve that.

Furthermore, it mandates Internet access in order to use it if I understand well and thus it may break offline installation.

So, README is interesting (and I really appreciate that you gave right documentation) but I'd like you to show me where you desperately needs this and why you cannot do it another way.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

See, there are two scripts:
generate.sh --> this one needs an internet access, but it is not a run-time script.
install.sh --> this is is a run-time script, and it doesn`t need internet access.

We intentionally seperated functionality into these two scripts to avoid internet dependency.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> I didn't looked at all the patch as I'm not sure to understand exactly why we need it and why we cannot use some ONAP APIs in order to achieve that.
> 
> Furthermore, it mandates Internet access in order to use it if I understand well and thus it may break offline installation.
> 
> So, README is interesting (and I really appreciate that you gave right documentation) but I'd like you to show me where you desperately needs this and why you cannot do it another way.

Sylvain,

Well, the whole idea here is to be able to generate the configMap, which contains 3GPP openAPI descriptions. These are changing ~3-4 times a year, and we`d like to have a possibility to load such openAPI descriptions to the relevant DCAE components, which might use them.

There are some openAPIs delivered already within the packages, but we`d like to make it easier for operators to prepare a configMap with updated openAPIs using a script (and not manually downloading and preparing a public/local URL map), then load the ready-made configMap with another one.

The target solution is openAPi onboarding in SDC, but this is something we could do only in Honolulu release. We agreed the solution with Vijay, a question is only, where to place these scripts, so they`re available to the operator for use.

Rgds,
Damian

Patch Set 2:

Thank you Damian for Your thorough explanation, I really appreciate it.

However, in my understanding (anyone please prove me wrong if that's the case) ONAP is a platform that is meant to be _independent_ of the underlying OS layer - Kubernetes being it's only and canonical driver. After reading the readme and the sentence "Script works and has been tested on Ubuntu 18.04 and Ubuntu 19.10 which covers actual ONAP OS environment" (I use RedHat/Centos) and Sylvain comment to it I not only support his comment but then I even think that this tool doesn't even belong to OOM project because it doesn't leverage on the underlying Kubernetes platform (runtime wise).

The external repository is in that case not the only dependency here then but the OS on which these scripts can run is also a constraint hence a dependency as such.. Even if the only script to be run on target env on which ONAP is deployed doesn't need any artifacts from Internet it's still constrained by OS it's compatible with. And it's not possible to test each corner cases here, in OS regard.

Hence my suggestions:
1.Treat these tool as auxiliary and put it in some other repository (one of integration repos? not sure).
2.Deploy it as a native kubernetes application, i.e. put all required artifacts to a relevant image (new one?) and allow running the tools on Kubernetes platform (as pod) on demand. In that case: no external dependencies needed, all artifacts contained in a local image and no constrain from scripts perspective on underlying OS. In real life I think that ONAP operator would like to run any such tool on target deployment in this scenario.

>

After reading the readme and the sentence "Script works and has been tested on Ubuntu 18.04 and Ubuntu 19.10 which covers actual ONAP OS environment" (I use RedHat/Centos) and Sylvain comment to it I not only support his comment but then I even think that this tool doesn't even belong to OOM project because it doesn't leverage on the underlying Kubernetes platform (runtime wise).
> 
> The external repository is in that case not the only dependency here then but the OS on which these scripts can run is also a constraint hence a dependency as such.. Even if the only script to be run on target env on which ONAP is deployed doesn't need any artifacts from Internet it's still constrained by OS it's compatible with. And it's not possible to test each corner cases here, in OS regard.
> 
> Hence my suggestions:
> 1.Treat these tool as auxiliary and put it in some other repository (one of integration repos? not sure).
> 2.Deploy it as a native kubernetes application, i.e. put all required artifacts to a relevant image (new one?) and allow running the tools on Kubernetes platform (as pod) on demand. In that case: no external dependencies needed, all artifacts contained in a local image and no constrain from scripts perspective on underlying OS. In real life I think that ONAP operator would like to run any such tool on target deployment in this scenario.

Hi Bartek,
Well, the sentence is related to the fact, that the script was tested on these Ubuntu versions.
We haven`t introduced any OS specifics in a script, and due to this, we decided to code it in bash, even though Python/Java will be easier, but I`d bring additional compatibility issues.

I understand Your comments about Kubernetes and containers, but as I wrote - the script is intended to be executed in preparations/design time phase, not in run-time.

This script`s goal is to simplify the configMap generation.
We can as well describe it with documentation, how to generate such a configMap, and I can assure You, it will eb an error-prone process. This is why the script exists.

All right, we tried to include this script in DCAE project, now in OOM project.
My proposal is to discuss this script location with:
* Vijay Venkatesh Kumar (DCAE PTL)
* Sylvain Desbureaux (OOM PTL)
... in thr next OOM weekly. Feel invited as well.
We need to decide, what is the best place to have that script stored.

We need to deliver it with ONAP, so the operators would be able to benefit from it.
Where we store it - it is negotiable. (Actually Vijay suggested OOM/contrib)...

Rgds,
Damian

> Where we store it - it is negotiable. (Actually Vijay suggested OOM/contrib)...
>

Yeah I did suggest OOM/contrib - sorry if thats not right place holder.

The scripts provide an auxillary feature to generate and update configmap for ONAP deployed components. For Guilin, its targetted for vescollector however this could apply for other components (beyond DCAE) in future needing external schemas (hence thought OOM might be better place holder to hold such scripts related to config-map management). 
However from Guilin standpoint, VESCollector container already includes required stddefined schema's hence these scripts are optional. Although I agree with Damian -  they do simplify and provide an option for operators to override configuration post deployment if required.

We can certainly discuss on next OOM meeting to determine right repo. 

Damian - On a side note, it will be good to syncup with ARC and TCC/ORAN coordinators to identify/define ONAP process for ingesting schemas/model from external community (such as 3gpp) for future release.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Hi Bartek,
> Well, the sentence is related to the fact, that the script was tested on these Ubuntu versions.
> We haven`t introduced any OS specifics in a script, and due to this, we decided to code it in bash, even though Python/Java will be easier, but I`d bring additional compatibility issues.
> 

by the way, Bash is GPLv3 so we're not allowed to use it. 
You can move to sh / dash / zsh (but then you'll need to check OS compatibility for the two last I guess) if you want but I think Bartek proposal (having a Job doing that) would be for the best no?

And I'm OK to ask for a new repo in order to embed scripts / docker generation + add something in "main OOM" in order to launch it.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > Hi Bartek,
> > Well, the sentence is related to the fact, that the script was tested on these Ubuntu versions.
> > We haven`t introduced any OS specifics in a script, and due to this, we decided to code it in bash, even though Python/Java will be easier, but I`d bring additional compatibility issues.
> > 
> 
> by the way, Bash is GPLv3 so we're not allowed to use it. 
> You can move to sh / dash / zsh (but then you'll need to check OS compatibility for the two last I guess) if you want but I think Bartek proposal (having a Job doing that) would be for the best no?
> 
> And I'm OK to ask for a new repo in order to embed scripts / docker generation + add something in "main OOM" in order to launch it.

Ok, we`d check with sh only.
We cannot use this as a job, as the intention is, that the operator prepares a configMap as he/she needs. If we`d put that script in the job, then we`d create an internet dependency, which we want to avoid.

Let`s discuss further in the OOM call on Wednesday.
